nodes.name: 山地森林
nodes.summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: Cundinamarca antpitta
nodes.summary: Cundinamarca antpitta（Cundinamarca antpitta，学名 Grallaria kaestneri），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，主要分布于次生林，常见栖息地包括山地森林，主要威胁包括森林砍伐、栖息地破坏。

nodes.name: 次生林
nodes.summary: 次生林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联749种鸟类，包括孤䳍、Sickle-winged guan、黑額鳴冠雉等749种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: However, it appears to have been extirpated from the northern part of its historical range. It inhabits humid montane forest and mature secondary woodland, where it greatly favors dense vegetation in the understorey

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: However, it appears to have been extirpated from the northern part of its historical range. It inhabits humid montane forest and mature secondary woodland, where it greatly favors dense vegetation in the understorey

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: "The species is threatened by the loss and degradation of its habitat, mainly as a consequence of logging, clearance for small-scale agriculture and grazing by goats

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The species has disappeared from completely cleared areas in the Farallondes de Medina and near Monterredondo." "However, selective logging may even favour the species, in contrast to clear-cutting, which is clearly a threat and has generally occurred up to altitudes of 1,500-2,000 m on the east slope." "[P]rojections of habitat loss as a consequence of deforestation suggest that between 2015 and 2040 the species may lose 46% of suitable habitat [so] the population may be declining by 20-29% over the next three generations." The Chingaza National Natural Park does host the Cundinamarca antpitta "but further protected areas will be crucial for the species' conservation and survival". Such a protected area, called Reserva Natural Refugio Tototoi, was created in 2023 by a partnership of the American Bird Conservancy, two Colombian non-governmental bodies, and a local family whose land adjoins the preserve
